title: United States Online Nonimmigrant Visa Application (Form DS-160)
description: Apply online for a U.S. nonimmigrant visa (temporary travel to the United States, including K fiancé(e) visas) via Form DS-160, submitted electronically through the Consular Electronic Application Center (CEAC) at ceac.state.gov/genniv. All nonimmigrant visa categories require a completed DS-160 before a consular interview is scheduled; consular officers use the submitted answers, combined with the interview, to determine visa eligibility. The live CEAC wizard requires an authenticated session tied to the applicant's chosen interview post and cannot be walked end-to-end without submitting a real identity claim into a live federal system, so this document is sourced from the Department of State's own OMB-cleared DS-160 form text (OMB control 1405-0182) plus its official FAQ and Enhanced Vetting social-media-collection notices, per the sourced-inventory technique (see VERIFICATION.md). This document models the core question set common to (or, for the Temporary Worker branch, conditionally triggered by) a standard nonimmigrant visa application: personal, address/contact, passport, travel, travel-companion, previous-U.S.-travel, U.S. point of contact, family, work/education/training, and security-and-background information, ending at electronic signature and submission. It does not submit the application, schedule the interview, collect the visa fee, or model the visa-classification-specific supplemental question sets other than a representative Temporary Worker branch (Student/Exchange Visitor SEVIS questions, Treaty Trader/Investor questions, Border Crossing Card questions, and K-visa-specific questions are out of scope); the live ceac.state.gov/genniv source is always authoritative.

authority.name: U.S. Department of State, Bureau of Consular Affairs

source.documentRef: Form DS-160 (OMB Control Number 1405-0182); DS-160 Frequently Asked Questions; Department of State 'Frequently Asked Questions on Social Media Identifiers in the DS-160 and DS-260' (June 4, 2019); ceac.state.gov/genniv

verification.notes: Source-derived reference schema authored using the sourced-inventory technique proven on nz/dia/realme-verified-identity (GOV-660): the live CEAC DS-160 wizard (ceac.state.gov/genniv) requires an authenticated, interview-post-specific session and cannot be walked without submitting a genuine identity claim into a live federal visa-processing system, so no live walkthrough was performed. Instead this document is derived from (1) the actual OMB-cleared DS-160 form text (OMB Control Number 1405-0182, form edition dated 2010-2011, retrieved from a law-firm-hosted reproduction of the printed/PDF rendering of the form fetched at authoring time and decoded field-by-field from the PDF's own text streams — not a paraphrase), which supplied the great majority of field labels, section order, and the verbatim 23-question Security and Background block current as of that edition; (2) travel.state.gov's own current DS-160 overview and FAQ pages, which confirmed the form is unchanged in structure and purpose and supplied the 'last five U.S. visits' / 'filed an immigrant petition' / browser-support details; and (3) the Department's own June 4, 2019 'Frequently Asked Questions on Social Media Identifiers in the DS-160 and DS-260' notice, a primary Enhanced Vetting policy document, which confirmed the post-2011 addition of social-media-identifier, additional-phone-number, and additional-email-address questions (all covering the preceding five years, passwords never requested). A small number of Security and Background sub-questions — the human-trafficking family-benefit criminal questions and the forced-abortion/sterilization and coerced-organ-transplantation security questions — are known, well-corroborated post-2011 additions to the live form but were only available from secondary compilations (not the primary OMB-cleared text), and are flagged individually in their `sourceRef` and in VERIFICATION.md rather than silently presented at the same confidence as the primary-sourced fields. Status remains `draft`, not `verified`: a full field-by-field pass against the live, authenticated CEAC wizard was not possible and the primary form text is a c. 2010-2011 edition, not a current live capture. See VERIFICATION.md for the complete source-confidence breakdown and out-of-scope list.

fields.name: surnames

fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Personal Information 1: Surnames

fields.name: givenNames
fields.label: Given names (as in passport)

fields.name: hasOtherNamesUsed
fields.label: Have you ever used other names (i.e., maiden, religious, professional, alias, etc.)?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Personal Information 1

fields.name: otherNamesUsed
fields.label: Other names used
fields.description: The live form collects up to four other surname/given-name pairs as separate boxes; collapsed here into one free-text field listing each other name used.

fields.name: fullNameInNativeAlphabet
fields.label: Full name in native alphabet
fields.description: Optional; the live form offers a 'Does Not Apply' checkbox for applicants whose name is not written in a non-Latin script, or who use the same alphabet as their passport's Latin transliteration.
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Personal Information 1: Full Name in Native Alphabet

fields.name: additionalPhoneNumbersUsedLastFiveYears
fields.label: Additional phone numbers used in the last five years
fields.description: Collapsed free-text list; the live form collects each additional number in its own box.

fields.name: usedAdditionalEmailAddressesLastFiveYears
fields.label: Have you used any additional email addresses in the last five years?

fields.name: hasUsedListedSocialMediaPlatforms
fields.label: Have you used any of the social media platforms listed below within the last five years?
fields.description: The live form names specific social media platforms (e.g. Facebook, Twitter/X, Instagram, among others) and asks, per platform, whether the applicant has used it in the preceding five years. Consular officers request only the social media identifier/handle, never a password. Effective 31 May 2019; applicants of certain diplomatic/NATO visa classes (A-1, A-2, C-2, C-3 except attendants/servants/personal employees of accredited officials, G-1 through G-4, NATO-1 through NATO-6) are exempt.
fields.sourceRef: Department of State 'Frequently Asked Questions on Social Media Identifiers in the DS-160 and DS-260' (June 4, 2019)

fields.name: socialMediaIdentifiers
fields.label: Social media platforms and identifiers/handles used
fields.description: Collapsed platform:handle pairs; the live form collects one identifier per listed platform. Never include a password — the Department has stated it does not request them.
fields.sourceRef: Department of State 'Frequently Asked Questions on Social Media Identifiers in the DS-160 and DS-260' (June 4, 2019)

fields.label: Do you have any specialized skills or training in firearms, explosives, nuclear, biological, or chemical experience?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Additional Work/Education/Training Information

fields.name: specializedSkillsExplanation
fields.label: Explain your specialized skills or training
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Additional Work/Education/Training Information

fields.name: paramilitaryVigilanteRebelInvolvement
fields.label: Have you been involved with a paramilitary, vigilante, rebel, guerrilla, or insurgent organization?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Additional Work/Education/Training Information

fields.name: paramilitaryExplanation
fields.label: Explain the paramilitary/vigilante/rebel/guerrilla/insurgent involvement
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Additional Work/Education/Training Information

fields.name: hasServedInMilitary
fields.label: Have you ever served in the military?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Additional Work/Education/Training Information

fields.name: militaryServiceHistory
fields.label: Military service history
fields.description: Collapsed list of country, branch of service, rank/position, military specialty, and service dates; the live form collects up to four instances of service individually.
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Additional Work/Education/Training Information

fields.name: hasCommunicableDiseaseOfPublicHealthSignificance
fields.label: Do you have a communicable disease of public health significance?
fields.description: Public health significance examples cited on the form include tuberculosis (TB).
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Security and Background: Medical and Health Information (verbatim from OMB-cleared form text)

fields.name: hasThreateningMentalOrPhysicalDisorder
fields.label: Do you have a mental or physical disorder that poses or is likely to pose a threat to the safety or welfare of yourself or others?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Security and Background: Medical and Health Information (verbatim from OMB-cleared form text)

fields.name: isOrHasBeenDrugAbuserOrAddict
fields.label: Are you or have you ever been a drug abuser or addict?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Security and Background: Medical and Health Information (verbatim from OMB-cleared form text)

fields.name: hasBeenArrestedOrConvicted
fields.label: Have you ever been arrested or convicted for any offense or crime, even though subject of a pardon, amnesty, or other similar action?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Security and Background: Criminal Information (verbatim from OMB-cleared form text)

fields.name: hasViolatedControlledSubstancesLaw
fields.label: Have you ever violated, or engaged in a conspiracy to violate, any law relating to controlled substances?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Security and Background: Criminal Information (verbatim from OMB-cleared form text)

fields.name: seeksProstitutionOrUnlawfulCommercializedVice
fields.label: Are you coming to the United States to engage in prostitution or unlawful commercialized vice, or have you been engaged in prostitution or procuring prostitutes within the past 10 years?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Security and Background: Criminal Information (verbatim from OMB-cleared form text)

fields.name: hasEngagedInOrSeeksMoneyLaundering
fields.label: Have you ever been involved in, or do you seek to engage in, money laundering?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Security and Background: Criminal Information (verbatim from OMB-cleared form text)

fields.name: hasCommittedOrConspiredHumanTrafficking
fields.label: Have you ever committed or conspired to commit a human trafficking offense in the United States or outside the United States?
fields.description: Post-2011 addition to the Criminal Information block (Trafficking Victims Protection Reauthorization Act-related grounds), not present in the primary 2010-2011 OMB-cleared form text used for the rest of this section; sourced from a secondary compilation, lower confidence than the sibling verbatim questions.
fields.sourceRef: Secondary compilation of current DS-160 security/background questions (immihelp.com), not independently confirmed against the live CEAC form

fields.name: hasKnowinglyAidedHumanTrafficker
fields.label: Have you ever knowingly aided, abetted, assisted, or colluded with an individual who has committed or conspired to commit a severe human trafficking offense in the United States or outside the United States?
fields.description: Post-2011 addition; see hasCommittedOrConspiredHumanTrafficking for the sourcing-confidence caveat.
fields.sourceRef: Secondary compilation of current DS-160 security/background questions (immihelp.com), not independently confirmed against the live CEAC form

fields.name: isFamilyOfTraffickerBenefitedFromTrafficking
fields.label: Are you the spouse, son, or daughter of an individual who has committed, conspired to commit, or been identified as engaging in trafficking, and have you, within the last five years, knowingly benefited from the trafficking activities?
fields.description: Post-2011 addition; the live form asks this as three near-identical variants (trafficker, presidentially-identified trafficker, controlled-substance trafficker), collapsed here into one field. See hasCommittedOrConspiredHumanTrafficking for the sourcing-confidence caveat.
fields.sourceRef: Secondary compilation of current DS-160 security/background questions (immihelp.com), not independently confirmed against the live CEAC form

fields.name: seeksEspionageSabotageExportControlViolationsOrIllegalActivity
fields.label: Do you seek to engage in espionage, sabotage, export control violations, or any other illegal activity while in the United States?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Security and Background: Security Information (verbatim from OMB-cleared form text)

fields.name: seeksOrHasEngagedInTerroristActivities
fields.label: Do you seek to engage in terrorist activities while in the United States, or have you ever engaged in terrorist activities?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Security and Background: Security Information (verbatim from OMB-cleared form text)

fields.name: hasProvidedSupportToTerroristsOrTerroristOrganizations
fields.label: Have you ever, or do you intend to, provide financial assistance or other support to terrorists or terrorist organizations?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Security and Background: Security Information (verbatim from OMB-cleared form text)

fields.name: isMemberOrRepresentativeOfTerroristOrganization
fields.label: Are you a member or representative of a terrorist organization?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Security and Background: Security Information (verbatim from OMB-cleared form text)

fields.name: hasParticipatedInGenocide
fields.label: Have you ever ordered, incited, committed, assisted, or otherwise participated in genocide?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Security and Background: Security Information (verbatim from OMB-cleared form text)

fields.name: hasParticipatedInTorture
fields.label: Have you ever ordered, incited, committed, assisted, or otherwise participated in torture?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Security and Background: Security Information (verbatim from OMB-cleared form text)

fields.name: hasParticipatedInExtrajudicialKillings
fields.label: Have you ordered, incited, committed, assisted, or otherwise participated in extrajudicial killings, political killings, or other acts of violence?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Security and Background: Security Information (verbatim from OMB-cleared form text)

fields.name: hasParticipatedInSevereReligiousFreedomViolations
fields.label: Have you, while serving as a government official, been responsible for or directly carried out, at any time, particularly severe violations of religious freedom?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Security and Background: Security Information (verbatim from OMB-cleared form text)

fields.name: hasParticipatedInForcedAbortionOrSterilization
fields.label: Have you ever been directly involved in the establishment or enforcement of population controls forcing a woman to undergo an abortion against her free choice, or a man or a woman to undergo sterilization against their free will?
fields.description: Post-2011 addition to the Security Information block, not present in the primary 2010-2011 OMB-cleared form text used for the rest of this section; sourced from a secondary compilation, lower confidence than the sibling verbatim questions.
fields.sourceRef: Secondary compilation of current DS-160 security/background questions (immihelp.com), not independently confirmed against the live CEAC form

fields.name: hasParticipatedInCoercedOrganTransplantation
fields.label: Have you ever been directly involved in the coercive transplantation of human organs or bodily tissue?
fields.description: Post-2011 addition; see hasParticipatedInForcedAbortionOrSterilization for the sourcing-confidence caveat.
fields.sourceRef: Secondary compilation of current DS-160 security/background questions (immihelp.com), not independently confirmed against the live CEAC form

fields.name: hasBeenSubjectOfRemovalOrDeportationHearing
fields.label: Have you ever been the subject of a removal or deportation hearing?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Security and Background: Immigration Law Violation Information (verbatim from OMB-cleared form text)

fields.name: hasSoughtImmigrationBenefitByFraudOrMisrepresentation
fields.label: Have you ever sought to obtain or assist others to obtain a visa, entry into the United States, or any other United States immigration benefit by fraud or willful misrepresentation or other unlawful means?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Security and Background: Immigration Law Violation Information (verbatim from OMB-cleared form text)

fields.name: hasFailedToAttendRemovalOrInadmissibilityHearing
fields.label: Have you ever failed to attend a hearing on removability or inadmissibility within the last five years?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Security and Background: Immigration Law Violation Information (verbatim from OMB-cleared form text)

fields.name: hasBeenUnlawfullyPresentOrViolatedVisaTerms
fields.label: Have you ever been unlawfully present, overstayed the amount of time granted by an immigration official, or otherwise violated the terms of a U.S. visa?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Security and Background: Immigration Law Violation Information (verbatim from OMB-cleared form text)

fields.name: hasWithheldCustodyOfUsCitizenChild
fields.label: Have you ever withheld custody of a U.S. citizen child outside the United States from a person granted legal custody by a U.S. court?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Security and Background: Miscellaneous Information (verbatim from OMB-cleared form text)

fields.name: hasVotedInUsInViolationOfLaw
fields.label: Have you voted in the United States in violation of any law or regulation?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Security and Background: Miscellaneous Information (verbatim from OMB-cleared form text)

fields.name: hasRenouncedUsCitizenshipToAvoidTaxation
fields.label: Have you ever renounced United States citizenship for the purpose of avoiding taxation?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Security and Background: Miscellaneous Information (verbatim from OMB-cleared form text)

fields.name: attendedPublicSchoolOnFStatusWithoutReimbursement
fields.label: Have you ever attended a public elementary school on student (F) status, or a public secondary school after November 30, 1996 without reimbursing the school?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Security and Background: Miscellaneous Information (verbatim from OMB-cleared form text)

fields.name: securityBackgroundExplanation
fields.label: Explanation for any 'Yes' answer above
fields.description: A YES answer does not automatically signify visa ineligibility, but may require the applicant to personally appear before a consular officer. The live form collects a separate explanation box beside each individual 'Yes' answer; collapsed here into one field covering all of them.
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Security and Background sections: 'If yes, explain.' (verbatim from OMB-cleared form text)

fields.name: temporaryWorkerEmployerName
fields.label: Name of employer (temporary worker)
fields.description: Collected only when the purpose of trip is temporary work.
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Temporary Worker Visa Information

fields.name: temporaryWorkerEmployerAddress
fields.label: Employer address (temporary worker)
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Temporary Worker Visa Information

fields.name: temporaryWorkerMonthlySalaryUsd
fields.label: Monthly salary in USD (temporary worker)
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Temporary Worker Visa Information: Enter monthly salary (in USD)

fields.name: hasPetitionReceiptNumber
fields.label: Do you have an application receipt/petition number?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Temporary Worker Visa Information

fields.name: petitionFilerName
fields.label: Name of person/company who filed the petition
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Temporary Worker Visa Information: Name of Person/Company who Filed Petition

fields.name: petitionReceiptNumber
fields.label: Application receipt/petition number
fields.description: Given by USCIS after the petition was filed at a USCIS Service Center; 13 characters, the first three of which are letters.
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Temporary Worker Visa Information: Application Receipt/Petition Number (e.g., ABC1234567890)

fields.name: wasAssistedByPreparer
fields.label: Did anyone assist you in filling out this application?
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Sign and Submit: Preparer of the Application

fields.name: preparerName
fields.label: Preparer's name
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Sign and Submit: Preparer Information

fields.name: preparerOrganizationName
fields.label: Preparer's organization name
fields.description: Optional; the live form offers a 'Does Not Apply' checkbox.
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Sign and Submit: Preparer Information

fields.name: preparerRelationshipToApplicant
fields.label: Preparer's relationship to you
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 — Sign and Submit: Preparer Information

fields.name: certifiesInformationTrueAndComplete
fields.label: I certify that I have read and understood all the questions set forth in this application, and the answers I have furnished are true and correct to the best of my knowledge and belief
fields.description: Electronic signature certification; fingerprints are electronically scanned on the day of the interview, at which the applicant certifies all information is accurate.
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 FAQ — Electronic Signature

fields.name: electronicSignatureFullName
fields.label: Electronic signature — full name
fields.sourceRef: DS-160 FAQ — Electronic Signature: 'Sign Application'
